revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:ايشياما ديرا]][[de:Ishiyama-dera]][[en:Ishiyama-dera]][[fa:ایشییاما-درا]][[fr:Ishiyama-dera]][[gan:石山寺]][[ja:石山寺]][[zh:石山寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Ishiyamadera45n4272.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tōji Shingon-shū|qid=Q10343918|1=Tōji Shingon-shū}}|prefecture={{ill|Otsu|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.96041944|N|135.905625|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Rōben|qid=Q720819|1=Rōben}}|deity={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|qid=Q11446712|1=Cintāmaṇicakra}}|part_of={{ill|Q11638516|qid=Q11638516|1=Q11638516}}; {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|qid=Q750721|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}; {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}}}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{multiple image | align = right| direction = horizontal | header = | header_align = left/right/center | header_background = | footer | footer_align = left/right/center | footer_background = | width = | image1 = Ishiyamadera45n4272.jpg | width1 = 200 | caption1 = Hondō (Main Hall) | alt1 = | image2 = Ishiyamadera29n4272.jpg | width2 = 200 | caption2 = Tahōtō (Pagoda) | alt2 = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ishiyama-dera'''|石山寺||"Stony Mountain Temple"}} is a [[Shingon]] {{ill|Temple|en|Temple|qid=Q44539|lt=Temple|1=Temple}} in [[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu]] in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}'s {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. This temple is the thirteenth of the [[:en:Kansai Kannon Pilgrimage|Kansai Kannon Pilgrimage]].
==History== [[File:Hiroshige Full moon over a mountain landscape.jpg|thumb|The autumn moon at Ishiyama (石山の秋月), by [[Hiroshige]]]] [[File:Ishiyama-dera, November 2016.jpg|thumb|At night during Koyo]] It was constructed around 747 CE, and is said to have been founded by {{ill|Rōben|en|Rōben|qid=Q720819|lt=Rōben|1=Rōben}}. The temple contains a number of cultural assets. The temple possesses two fragments of manuscripts of the {{ill|Shiji|en|Shiji|qid=Q272530|lt=''Records of the Grand Historian'' (''Shiji'' 史記)|1=Shiji}}, the first of China's 24 dynastic histories, which are the only known extant fragments that pre-date the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} (618 - 907). According to literature available at the temple complex, the guardian carvings at Sanmon/Todaimon are by {{ill|Tankei|en|Tankei|qid=Q2668015|lt=Tankei|1=Tankei}} and {{ill|Unkei|en|Unkei|qid=Q2006048|lt=Unkei|1=Unkei}}. Allegedly, {{ill|Murasaki Shikibu|en|Murasaki Shikibu|qid=Q81731|lt=Murasaki Shikibu|1=Murasaki Shikibu}} began writing ''{{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|qid=Q8269|lt=The Tale of Genji|1=The Tale of Genji}}'' at Ishiyama-dera during a full moon night in August 1004. In commemoration, the temple maintains a Genji room featuring a life-size figure of Lady Murasaki and displays a statue in her honor.<ref>{{cite web|title=Ishiyama-dera Temple|url=http://www.taleofgenji.org/ishiyama.html|publisher=Taleofgenji.org|access-date=28 February 2012}}</ref>
The temple features as "The Autumn Moon at Ishiyama" ({{lang|ja|石山の秋月}} ''{{transl|ja|Ishiyama no Shūgetsu}}'') in the ''{{ill|Eight Views of Ōmi|en|Eight Views of Ōmi|qid=Q340790|lt=Eight Views of Ōmi|1=Eight Views of Ōmi}}'' thematic series in art and literature; examples include [[ukiyo-e]] prints by {{ill|Suzuki Harunobu|en|Suzuki Harunobu|qid=Q360978|lt=Harunobu|1=Suzuki Harunobu}} in the 18th century and [[Hiroshige]] in the 19th century.
==See also== * [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]] - For an explanation of the terms on Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture. * {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|qid=Q3245523|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)}} * {{ill|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (ancient documents)|en|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (ancient documents)|qid=Q7883225|lt=en:List of National Treasures of Japan (ancient documents)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (ancient documents)}} * [[:en:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (writings)|en:List of National Treasures of Japan (writings)]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Sources== *{{Commons category-inline|Ishiyamadera}} *{{ill|Kōjien|en|Kōjien|qid=Q1192611|lt=Kōjien|1=Kōjien}}, 5th edition
